title: High-Protein Tiramisu
tagline: Hung curd + vanilla whey cream, Marie Gold biscuits soaked in espresso, dusted with cocoa. No-bake.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients — the vanilla whey plus hung curd is what pushes this to ~28 g protein per serving, rare for a dessert.
Protein boost is already built in (whey). To push it higher, use a second half-scoop of whey and thin with a splash of milk.

Biscuits are the calorie lever: 6 Marie Gold instead of 8 saves ~40 kcal across the batch. For a harder cut, swap some biscuits for a toasted whole-wheat bread slice cut into fingers and dipped in coffee.

items: 15-20 ml of the espresso (the rest is for soaking)
Sweetener only if needed: 1-2 tsp honey or a little stevia/erythritol
1-2 tsp milk if the cream is too thick
1 espresso shot (30-40 ml) OR 1 tsp instant coffee + 35 ml hot water, cooled

steps.title: Brew and cool the coffee
steps.body: Pull 1 espresso shot (or mix 1 tsp instant coffee with 35 ml hot water) and let it cool completely. This matters — hot coffee dissolves Marie biscuits into soup instantly. Split it: keep ~15-20 ml for the cream, the rest for soaking.

steps.title: Make the coffee cream
steps.body: Whisk 300 g hung curd with 1 scoop (30 g) vanilla whey, 1/4 tsp vanilla, a pinch of salt, and 15-20 ml of the espresso until smooth. Adding coffee to the cream (not just the soak) masks the whey twang and makes it taste properly tiramisu-like. Taste and add 1-2 tsp honey only if needed. If too thick add 1-2 tsp milk; if loose, chill 10-15 min. You want a thick frosting texture.

steps.title: Soak the biscuits
steps.body: Dip each Marie Gold in the remaining coffee for just 0.5-1 second per side — they have the structural integrity of wet cardboard, so any longer and they collapse. (Or brush the coffee on instead.)

steps.title: Layer it up
steps.body: In a lunchbox-sized container, lay a base of soaked biscuits, spread over half the cream, add another biscuit layer, then the rest of the cream. If you only manage a single thick layer that's fine — tiramisu is forgiving and it becomes more of a tiramisu pudding.

steps.title: Dust with cocoa
steps.body: Dust the top generously with 1-2 tsp cocoa powder (and a little between layers if you want stronger chocolate notes).

steps.title: Chill
steps.body: Refrigerate at least 2 hours, ideally overnight. This is the most important step — fresh it tastes like a yogurt shake, but chilled the coffee blooms, cocoa softens, whey integrates and the biscuits go cake-like.

notes: Coffee in the cream AND the soak beats coffee-in-soak-only for this protein version — it distributes the flavour and hides the whey taste. Don't dump the whole shot into the cream though, or it goes runny.
Going biscuit-heavy makes it too dense — Marie Gold absorbs moisture aggressively, so it balances out after an overnight chill. For best texture aim for roughly 1 part biscuit to 2 parts cream.
Optional upgrades: 5 g cream cheese for authentic tang, a pinch of cinnamon, or a few drops of coffee essence.
Protein desserts always taste better the next day — don't judge it right after assembly.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
